reputationCondition.archetypesHint: This condition is only offered to Major characters with one of the selected archetypes.
reputationCondition.archetypesUniversal: No archetype selected — this condition is offered to characters of every archetype.

innerConflict.completedNote: This conflict is resolved. GM may reopen.

magicSkill.descriptionPlaceholder: What does this skill do, in fiction? (Click ✎ to edit.)
magicSkill.referenceUrlHint: Optional URL to source material — rulebook page, fan wiki, house-rules doc.
magicSkill.castNote: Triggers VFX and sound on all clients.

randomEvent.archetypeHint: Only Major characters with this archetype can be targeted by this event.
randomEvent.positiveHint: On a successful roll, the player picks one of these as a positive reputation tag.
randomEvent.negativeHint: On a failed roll, the player picks one of these as a negative reputation tag.

npc.descriptionPlaceholder: Brief sketch of this NPC. (Click ✎ to edit.)
npc.promoteConfirm: Promote {name} to Connection? They'll gain a resolve track, impressions, and a theme. Bio, description, scene info, and personas are preserved.
npc.promoteSuccess: {name} has been promoted to a Connection actor.
npc.promoteFailed: Failed to create the Connection actor. Please try again.

family.criteriaPlaceholder: A daughter of this house is judged by…
family.criteriaNote: — shown read-only on every member Major's tab
family.noMembers: No Major characters linked to this family yet.
family.linkMajorSelect: Select a Major character to add to this family.
family.noMajorsToLink: No unlinked Major characters found in this world.

cameo.pickTokenTitle: Click to upload or pick a token image

connection.descriptionPlaceholder: Sketch this connection in two or three sentences. (Click ✎ to edit.)
connection.hoverSummaryPlaceholder: What appears when a player mouses over this connection's token.
connection.hoverSubtitlePlaceholder: Short italic subtitle (e.g., "Lady Hetherington's lady's maid")
connection.addImpressionMajor: Which Major character does this Connection have an impression of?
connection.noMajorsForImpression: No Major characters exist in this world yet.
connection.editLinkedMajorPrompt: Which Major character is this Connection tied to? (Choose "(no Major)" to clear.)

public.criteriaEmpty: No reputation criteria set for this family.
public.innerConflictRule: complete at 6 total or 5 on one side

private.noConnections: Drag a Connection here, or click + to create one.

dossier.blankConnectionPage: click another connection chip to view alongside
dossier.trueIdentityThemeHint: The dossier's visual theme when no persona is active. To assign a different theme to a persona, edit the persona.
dossier.addConditionHelp: Author a custom reputation condition and grant it to this character. Conditions are visible to all players when active.
dossier.conditionDescriptionPlaceholder: What this condition represents — mechanics, fiction, or both.
dossier.noConditionsGMHint: No active conditions. Click "+ add condition" to grant one.
dossier.removeConditionConfirm: Remove the condition "{name}" from this character?
dossier.desirePlaceholder: What does this character want, in this moment? (Click ✎ to edit.)
dossier.backstoryPlaceholder: The character's history, in their own voice. (Click ✎ to edit. Use HTML <hr> tags to split into multi-page spreads.)

reputationCriteria.body: Family-specific judgment the world applies to all members of this house. Set on the Family sheet — read-only here.

reputationTags.body: Adjectives the world has begun to associate with this character. Three of one polarity triggers a Reputation Condition.

activeConditions.body: Lasting social consequences earned by reputation. Each shapes how the world treats this character until lifted.

innerConflict.body: Two opposing pressures. Mark a box on either side as your character acts. Completes at 6 total or 5 on one side.

completedConflicts.body: Conflicts this character has resolved. Each grants an Expanded Backstory Action — a tool for shaping the story.

desire.body: What this character wants this cycle. Drives Novel Chapter scenes. May change at Upkeep when a desire-arc wraps.

notesObjectives.body: Player-authored running notes. What is this character paying attention to? What would they like to accomplish next?

connections.body: Other characters this person has formed relationships with. Click any to open their sheet.

backstory.body: The character's history. May grow over time as Expanded Backstory Actions reveal new chapters.

magicSkills.body: Homebrew abilities tied to family bloodline or training. Casting plays an on-screen effect; some trigger persona changes.

adventurerSentiment.body: Pure flavor field. How does this character regard adventurers, freelancers, and the questing rabble?

connectionDescription.body: This connection's bio and personality. Authored by the GM; share ownership with the linked Major's player when appropriate.

impressions.body: What this connection thinks of each Major they've encountered. Shapes how they behave when scenes unfold.

linkedMajor.body: The Major character this connection primarily belongs to. Most connections have one; some are shared.

connectionResolve.body: This connection's smaller resolve track. Default 1 of 5. Used in scenes where they act on their own interests.

personas.body: Alternate identities. Each carries its own name, portrait, and visibility overrides. Switch via the picker.

familyMotto.body: The family's sworn declaration — read aloud at formal toasts and printed on the crest.

familyOrigin.body: The family's social standing: royalty, nobility, gentry, commoner, or impoverished.

archetype.body: The character's narrative archetype — the role they play in the story. Drives the pool of random events the GM can launch at this character.

peerage.body: This character's social standing: royalty, nobility, gentry, commoner, or impoverished. Distinct from archetype.

heirStatus.body: Whether the family has a named heir, and in what form. Affects reputation conditions in some scenarios.

uniqueNegativeRepCriteria.body: The standard by which this family's members are judged. Shown read-only on every member Major's Public tab.

familyNotes.body: GM-only history, internal politics, and secrets the players don't know.

memberMajors.body: Major Characters linked to this family. Set a Major's familyId field to add them here.

npcDescription.body: Bio, role, and backstory for this NPC. Longer prose goes here; the Hover Summary is the one-line version for the canvas.

publicTags.body: Short tags visible on the canvas hover card and in the NPC Organizer. Examples: eligible, tradesman, widowed, spy.

hoverSummary.body: The one or two sentences that appear when a player hovers over this character's token on the canvas.

tagPolarity.body: Positive (▲) for desirable traits, Negative (▼) for risky ones. Three of one polarity triggers a Reputation Condition.

tagDescription.body: What this tag means in play. Helps the GM and table understand the tag's narrative weight.

tagSource.body: Where this tag came from — the scene or moment of play that earned it. Shows in the Upkeep review.

conditionActive.body: Whether this Reputation Condition is currently in effect. Auto-deactivates if source tag count drops below 3.

conditionSourceTags.body: The reputation tags that triggered this condition. Removing one may deactivate the condition.

innerConflictLabel.body: The two abstract pressures pulling this character apart — Family vs. Independence, Ambition vs. Love, etc.

magicVfxKey.body: The Sequencer/JB2A asset path that plays when this skill is cast. Optional — falls back to a chat card if missing.

magicHidden.body: Whether this skill is hidden from the Public Info dashboard. Useful for secret abilities.

magicPersonaSwap.body: Casting this skill also switches the character to the named persona. Used for transformation skills like Alter Self.

backstorySource.body: The Inner Conflict whose resolution earned this Backstory Action. Read-only — links to the source conflict.

backstoryStatus.body: Whether this action has been expanded into play and whether it has been used yet.

resolveTrack.body: A character's determination to shape the story. Spend to do something difficult, harm another's interests, or compel them.

mtToggle.body: The Major Threshold marker. Spent to introduce or shape large narrative consequences.

monologueDot.body: Each cycle, this character may take one Inner Monologue — a scene revealing their true thoughts. Cleared at Upkeep.

personaSwitch.whisperNote: whispered to GM and player only

s1.heading: Trade Reputation Tags
s1.body: During any scene you share with another Major Character, you may offer to trade a Reputation Tag — giving one of your tags to them, or receiving one of theirs.
s1.positive: Positive tags can only be traded for positive tags.
s1.negative: Negative tags can only be traded for negative tags.

s2.heading: Spend Resolve Tokens
s2.intro: Spend one Resolve Token to do any of the following:
s2.bullet1: Erase one of your Reputation Tags (any polarity).
s2.bullet2: Prevent a Negative Reputation Tag from being placed on you.
s2.bullet3: Claim a second action in a scene.
s2.bullet4: Take a Monologue (once per cycle — see section 6).
s2.noToken: You cannot spend a token you don't have. Check the strip before spending.

s3.heading: Negotiate a Scene Outcome
s3.triggers: Any player may call for negotiation when a scene outcome would place a Reputation Tag on their character.
s3.responses: The other player(s) involved must respond: they may agree, modify the tag's wording, or refuse.
s3.refused: If refused, the original tag stands. You may spend a Resolve Token to prevent it entirely.

s4.heading: Earn Reputation Tags
s4.bullet1: Perform an action witnessed by others in a public scene.
s4.bullet2: Declare an intention that another character can report on.
s4.bullet3: Be the subject of a rumour (Rumour & Scandal phase).
s4.bullet4: Receive a tag from another character via trade.

s5.heading: Trigger a Condition
s5.body: When you accumulate 3 or more Reputation Tags of the same polarity, a Condition is triggered. The facilitator and the player choose one together from the available list.
s5.noCancel: Conditions cannot be removed mid-phase. They clear during Upkeep if the triggering tag count drops below 3.

s6.heading: Take a Monologue
s6.body: Once per cycle, spend a Resolve Token to take a Monologue: a private in-character reflection that only your player reads aloud. The monologue dot on your strip marks it spent. It resets at the start of each new cycle.

monologueEditor.title: Inner Monologue
monologueEditor.placeholder: What does your character truly feel, in this moment…
monologueEditor.alreadySpent: You have already taken your monologue this cycle.
monologueEditor.emptyWarning: Write something before posting your monologue.

dock.title: My Characters
dock.empty: No characters yet. Open the actors sidebar to create one.

revealControl.title: REVEAL CONTROL
revealControl.consequencePublic: This will reveal {name}'s {field} to all players. The reveal posts a chat card and is logged. Continue?
revealControl.consequenceRedacted: This will replace {name}'s {field} with a redaction bar visible to all players. Continue?
revealControl.consequenceSecret: This will hide {name}'s {field} from all players.

npcQuickCreate.namePlaceholder: Leave blank to use role as name

hud.advanceConfirmWrap: End cycle and advance from {current} to {next} (Cycle {cycle})?
hud.advanceConfirmEnd: Advance from {current} to {next}? This will end the game.
hud.advancePostedSkip: Cycle advanced to: {phase}. ({skipped} skipped — final cycle.)
hud.finalCycleBadgeHint: This is the final cycle. Rumour & Scandal and the second Reputation phase are skipped; the second Epistolary serves as the epilogue.
hud.finalCycleToggleHint: Mark this as the final cycle. Skips Rumour & Scandal and the second Reputation phase; the second Epistolary becomes the epilogue.
hud.finalCyclePosted: The Facilitator declares this the final cycle of the story.
hud.finalCycleClearedPosted: The Facilitator unmarks the final cycle. Play continues.
hud.gmOnlyAdvance: Only the Facilitator can advance the cycle phase.

npcOrganizer.empty: No character actors yet. Create one with the buttons above.
npcOrganizer.footerHint: drag a row onto the canvas to place a token · double-click to open · right-click for more
npcOrganizer.onSceneHint: A token for this character is on the active scene.
npcOrganizer.createFailed: Could not create the actor. See the console for details.

ctx.removeConfirm: Remove all tokens of {name} from this scene? The actor is not deleted.
ctx.deleteActorConfirm: Delete the actor "{name}" permanently? Any tokens of theirs will also be removed.

publicInfoCard.empty: Nothing has been made public for this character yet.

dashboard.title: Public Info · Facilitator Dashboard
dashboard.subtitle: All Major characters at a glance · GM controls below · individual sheets remain authoritative.
dashboard.subtitlePlayer: All Major characters at a glance · individual sheets remain authoritative.
dashboard.noMajors: No Major characters yet. Create one in the actors sidebar.

bulk.refreshResolveHint: Reset every Major's resolve to the default starting value.
bulk.refreshResolveConfirm: Reset resolve to {n} for all Major characters?
bulk.refreshResolvePosted: Resolve refreshed to {n} for {count} Major characters.
bulk.clearMonologuesHint: Clear the monologue-spent flag on all Majors for a new cycle.
bulk.clearMonologuesConfirm: Clear monologue flags for all Major characters?
bulk.clearMonologuesPosted: Monologue flags cleared for {count} Major characters.
bulk.revealDesiresHint: Toggle all Major desires between public and secret.
bulk.revealDesireConfirm: Make all Major desires public? This is a dramatic move — the table will see them.
bulk.revealDesirePosted: All Major desires are now public.
bulk.hideDesireConfirm: Hide all Major desires? They will return to secret.
bulk.hideDesirePosted: All Major desires are now secret.
bulk.advancePhaseHint: Advance the world cycle to the next phase.
bulk.advancePhaseConfirm: Advance to the next cycle phase?
bulk.permissionsLinkHint: Open the Bulk Permissions panel to set actor ownership for all users.

bulkPermissions.colQuickSetHint: Set all actors for this user
bulkPermissions.rowQuickSetHint: Set all users for this actor
bulkPermissions.strandedHint: No player has owner access to this actor
bulkPermissions.noActors: No actors found in this world.

section.resetHint: Reset this section to default permission levels
section.resetConfirm: Reset all {section} permissions to default ({level})? This overwrites any explicit grants.

footer.saveConfirm: Apply {n} permission change(s)? Ownership will update immediately.

tooltipsEnabled.name: Show Rule Tooltips
tooltipsEnabled.hint: Hover any section header (marked with a small ?) to see a paraphrased rule explanation. Disable to hide the ? glyphs and suppress all tooltip hovers.

upkeepWizardEnabled.name: Enable Upkeep Wizard
upkeepWizardEnabled.hint: When the cycle advances to Upkeep, open a guided wizard for each of your Major characters. Disable to skip the wizard and handle Upkeep manually on the character sheet.

applyFoundryChrome.name: Apply Foundry Chrome Theme
applyFoundryChrome.hint: Re-themes Foundry's surrounding chrome (titlebars, sidebar, chat log, scene navigation, form controls, notifications, player list, hotbar) to match the Good Society house style. Toggles instantly without a page reload.

hoverCardEnabled.name: Show Token Hover Card
hoverCardEnabled.hint: When enabled, hovering a token on canvas shows the Good Society themed hover card. Disable to use Foundry's default tooltip instead.

hoverCardMajorAutoSummary.name: Auto-Summarize Major Hover Card
hoverCardMajorAutoSummary.hint: When enabled, hovering a Major's token shows an auto-derived snapshot (active condition, top reputation tags, family criteria). Disable to show only the name + archetype subtitle.

applyWorldIdentity.name: Apply World Identity Surfaces
applyWorldIdentity.hint: Activates the Arrival (empty-canvas state) and the system pause overlay. Disable to revert to Foundry's default empty canvas and pause overlay.

arrivalEnabled.name: Show the Arrival
arrivalEnabled.hint: When enabled, fills the canvas with a themed splash whenever no scene is active. Disable to leave the canvas blank.

arrivalTitle.name: Arrival Title
arrivalTitle.hint: The text displayed on the Arrival splash. Defaults to "Welcome to Good Society" — override with your world's name (e.g., "Welcome to Swan's Crossing").

arrivalBackgroundUrl.name: Arrival Background
arrivalBackgroundUrl.hint: Optional image OR video file used as the Arrival's backdrop. Video files (mp4, webm, mov, m4v, ogv) play silently on loop. Falls back to a dark gradient when empty.

arrivalCornerOrnamentUrl.name: Arrival Corner Ornament
arrivalCornerOrnamentUrl.hint: Optional decorative asset rendered in each corner of the Arrival (and at half size on the pause overlay). Single asset, mirrored per corner.

arrivalVideoPlaybackRate.name: Arrival Video Speed
arrivalVideoPlaybackRate.hint: Playback rate for the Arrival video backdrop. 1.0 is real-time; 0.5 is half-speed (ambient); 0.25 is very slow. No effect on still-image backgrounds.

arrivalShowTitle.name: Show Arrival Title
arrivalShowTitle.hint: When enabled, the centred 'Welcome' title and gilt rule render over the Arrival backdrop. Turn off to let the video / image play uninterrupted.

pauseCameoImageUrl.name: Pause Cameo Image
pauseCameoImageUrl.hint: Optional 72-px image rendered at the centre of the pause overlay. Falls back to the world's first letter as a monogram.

phaseSplashEnabled.name: Phase-Change Splash & Sound
phaseSplashEnabled.hint: When enabled, advancing the cycle closes the active scene, plays a page-turn sound, and shows a phase-specific splash screen (Reputation, Rumour & Scandal, Epistolary, Upkeep). Novel phases use the default Arrival screen.

applyChromeIcons.name: Use Custom Chrome Icons
applyChromeIcons.hint: Replaces Foundry's default icons on scene controls, sidebar tabs, and the Cabinet rail with the system's illustrated icons. Requires the chrome theme to be enabled. Falls back to Foundry's defaults for any surface without a custom asset.

cabinetEnabled.name: Show the Cabinet
cabinetEnabled.hint: An on-theme rail for collapsing system surfaces, third-party module UIs, and Foundry chrome at once. Anchored above the macro hotbar. The drawer also exposes GM-only launchers for the NPC Organizer, Bulk Permissions panel, Session Log, Calendar, Letter Composer, and Rumour Board.

playerSidebarFilter.name: Narrow Sidebar for Players
playerSidebarFilter.hint: Hides Combat, Scenes, Journal, Tables, Cards, Compendium, and Settings tabs from the Foundry sidebar for non-GM users. Players keep Chat, Actors, Items, Macros, and Playlists. Has no effect for GMs. The Novel Reader replaces the Journal tab as the player-facing archive.

monologueOverlayEnabled.name: Monologue Scene-Freeze Overlay
monologueOverlayEnabled.hint: When a player spends a monologue token, all clients render a full-viewport scene-freeze overlay. Disable to fall back to the chat-card-only flow.

archiveMonologuesToJournal.name: Archive Monologues to the Journal
archiveMonologuesToJournal.hint: When enabled, every monologue is archived as a JournalEntry under "Monologues / Cycle N". Disable to skip the archive step.

resolveHandoffAnimationEnabled.name: Animate Resolve Handoff
resolveHandoffAnimationEnabled.hint: When enabled, a resolve token handed across the table tweens visually from the spender's tracker to the recipient's. Actor updates and chat card still fire when off.

novelTitle.name: Novel Title
novelTitle.hint: The title of your collaboratively-written novel (per the rulebook's end-of-game ritual, p.115). Defaults to the world title until you set this.

novelReaderEnabled.name: Show the Novel Reader
novelReaderEnabled.hint: Enables the dedicated reader app for browsing letters, monologues, session logs, and cycle reflections as one chronological archive.

autoCreateCycleDividers.name: Auto-Create Cycle Reflections
autoCreateCycleDividers.hint: On the first transition into Upkeep each cycle, automatically create a "Cycle N — Reflections" journal entry summarizing the cycle's events.

autoRefreshOnUpkeep.name: Auto-Refresh Resolve on Upkeep
autoRefreshOnUpkeep.hint: When the cycle advances to Upkeep, visually highlight the Refresh Resolve button on the dashboard. GM still clicks — this never fires automatically.

promptOnThreeTags.name: Prompt on Three Reputation Tags
promptOnThreeTags.hint: When a Major reaches three tags of one polarity, open a reminder to review Reputation Conditions.

defaultMaxResolve.name: Default Maximum Resolve
defaultMaxResolve.hint: The maximum resolve track length for new Major and Connection characters. Default: 5.

defaultStartingResolve.name: Default Starting Resolve
defaultStartingResolve.hint: The resolve value assigned to new Major characters, and the value used by Refresh Resolve on the dashboard. Default: 3.

homebrewMagicEnabled.name: Enable Homebrew Magic & Skills
homebrewMagicEnabled.hint: Show the Magic & Skills tab on Major character sheets and enable VFX casting. Disable for tables playing canonical Good Society without the homebrew additions.

organizerPlayerVisible.name: NPC Organizer Visible to Players
organizerPlayerVisible.hint: When enabled, players can open the NPC Organizer sidebar from the scene controls. By default only the GM can see it.

isFinalCycle.name: Final cycle (skip Rumour & Scandal + 2nd Reputation)
isFinalCycle.hint: When enabled, the cycle skips Rumour & Scandal (position 3) and the second Reputation phase (position 6); the second Epistolary becomes the epilogue. Per Good Society rulebook p.114-115. Toggleable from the Cycle HUD too.

reputationPhaseWizardEnabled.name: Reputation Phase Wizard
reputationPhaseWizardEnabled.hint: When enabled, automatically opens the Reputation Phase Wizard when the phase advances to Reputation. GM only.

personaEditor.primaryHint: Toggle to make this the primary identity. One persona can be primary.
personaEditor.hoverSummaryPlaceholder: What players see when hovering this persona's token…
personaEditor.chatColorHint: Overrides the character theme's brand color for chat cards posted as this persona.
personaEditor.themeHint: The dossier's visual theme when this persona is active. Select "inherit true identity" to use the actor's own theme.
personaEditor.deletePrimaryHint: Set another persona as primary before deleting this one.
personaEditor.deleteConfirm: Delete the persona "{name}"? Chat history attributed to it is preserved.

personaSwitcher.triggerHint: Click to switch persona or add a new one
personaSwitcher.themeSelectHint: Character theme — changes palette and typography
personaSwitcher.swapFailed: Persona swap failed. See the console for details.

sceneId.label: Destination scene
sceneId.hint: The scene the table travels to when a token moves into this region.

confirm.label: Confirm before travelling
confirm.hint: Ask the Gamemaster to confirm before the scene changes. Leave off for instant travel.

scenePortal.missingScene: Scene portal: the destination scene no longer exists. Edit the region behavior to pick a new one.
scenePortal.confirmBody: Activate the scene "{scene}" and bring the whole table to it?

conditionsCompendium.gmOnly: Only the Gamemaster can manage the reputation conditions compendium.
conditionsCompendium.created: Created a "Reputation Conditions" compendium. Add reputation-condition items to it.
conditionsCompendium.createFailed: Could not create the reputation conditions compendium — see the console for details.

resetCampaign.gmOnly: Only the Gamemaster can reset the campaign.
resetCampaign.title: Reset Campaign to Cycle 1?
resetCampaign.confirmBody: Bring the world back to a fresh Cycle 1, keeping every character intact but clearing everything that happened.
resetCampaign.bullet2: All chat messages, rumours, session events, and calendar events deleted
resetCampaign.bullet3: System journals (letters, monologues, session log, cycle reflections) deleted; your own journals are kept
resetCampaign.bullet4: Every Major + Connection: resolve and Monologue Token refreshed, reputation tags and conditions cleared, inner conflicts reset, backstory actions marked unused
resetCampaign.confirmFooter: Characters, families, personas, magic skills, and conflict/backstory definitions are kept. This cannot be undone.
resetCampaign.error: Reset failed — see the console for details.
resetCampaign.success: Reset complete: Cycle 1, {majors} Major(s) and {connections} Connection(s) refreshed.

cabinet.title: The Cabinet
cabinet.subtitle: open and close everything you have on the board

resolve.noResolveToSpend: {name} has no resolve tokens to spend.

monologue.alreadyMonologued: This character has already monologued this cycle.
monologue.bannerInProgress: An inner monologue is in progress.
monologue.spenderWatching: You played your monologue token — {target} is responding.
monologue.audienceCannotDismiss: The monologue must end before you can close this overlay. The spender or GM must finish it.
monologue.noEligibleTargets: Every Major has already monologued this cycle. The pool refreshes during Upkeep.
monologue.noOwnedMajor: You don't have a Major character to play a monologue token from.
monologue.allSpent: Every Major you control has already played their monologue token this cycle. Tokens refresh at Upkeep.
monologue.pickSpenderLabel: Which character is playing their monologue token?

cycleDivider.heading: Cycle {cycle}
cycleDivider.noEvents: No events recorded yet — write what passed.

phaseSummary.rumourTitle: End of the Rumour & Scandal Phase
phaseSummary.intro: This is what the rest of the table is about to see. Edit any of it before posting, or cancel to skip the public recap.
phaseSummary.editHint: Click into the box below to edit. Formatting (lists, italics) is preserved.
phaseSummary.skippedNotice: Phase recap skipped. The phase has still completed.

novelPhase.subhead: Scenes happen during the Novel chapter. Aim for moments that change something.

bullet.desire: Bring a desire to every scene. Your character wants something. Let it surface, or let it collide with the world.
bullet.reputation: Lean into your reputation. Spend Resolve to land a tag on someone. Acknowledge the tags people already wear.
bullet.rumours: Pay attention to rumours. Spread what is already circulating, and let what is whispered shape what a scene means.
bullet.monologue: One inner monologue per cycle. When a beat hits, take the moment. Once spent, it is gone until Upkeep.
bullet.connections: Talk to Connections. They notice things Majors miss, and they remember.
bullet.choices: Make choices that matter. When you could end a scene early on a real beat, do.

pregame.heading: A quick reference for how the system works.
pregame.intro: Six things to know as you settle into your character. You can reopen this anytime from the Cabinet.
pregame.reopenHint: Open this again from the Cabinet whenever you need it.

desire.title: What your character wants
desire.body:
Your Desire is the main thing driving your character through the story. It appears in italics on the right page of your dossier.

Every scene should test, advance, complicate, or threaten it. If a scene does not engage your desire, ask whether your character should be in it.

You can revise your desire during Upkeep between cycles. For now, commit to it.

family.title: The people in your character's orbit
family.body:
Your Family appears read-only on your dossier. Its Reputation Criteria is the standard society uses to judge every family member. You do not author it; your character lives under it.

Your Connections are non-Major characters with a real relationship to your character: a parent, a confidant, a rival servant, anyone whose opinion matters. You will roleplay them when no other Major is in the scene.

Click any Connection chip on the right page to see what they think of you and the other Majors.

reputation.title: Tags become conditions
reputation.body:
You start with no Reputation Tags. Characters earn tags as scenes play out: ▲ for positive ("capable," "charming") or ▼ for negative ("reckless," "uncouth"). Tags are traded in scenes, awarded at the end, and reviewed during the Reputation Phase.

When you reach three tags of one polarity, a Reputation Condition triggers. Conditions are more permanent labels that affect how the world treats you, such as "Beloved" or "Disgraced." The condition clears if you drop below three tags.

The GM can also grant or revoke conditions directly on your dossier.

innerConflict.title: Two values held in tension
innerConflict.body:
Your Inner Conflict names two values your character holds in tension, such as Duty vs Freedom, Family vs Love, or Honor vs Survival. Each side has five boxes.

Fill a box when an action you take leans that way. The conflict resolves when you reach six boxes total or five boxes on one side. At that point your character has chosen, and the unfilled side closes off.

Conflicts are public on your dossier. They are meant to be felt by the table, not hidden.

tokens.title: Resolve, Major Token, and Monologue
tokens.body:
Resolve Tokens (the row of pips on your strip) are the fuel for offering tags and pushing back in scenes. Default is 3 of 5. They refresh at Upkeep.

MT (Major Token): one per cycle. Spend it to anchor a major moment, such as a binding negotiation or a decisive scene.

Monologue Token (the dot next to MT): one per cycle. Spend it for a private inner-voice scene that the table watches but does not interrupt.

All three refresh during Upkeep at the close of each cycle.

personas.title: Secondary identities
personas.body:
Your character may have Personas: secondary identities such as a secret alter-ego, a formal court face, or a disguised traveler. When a persona is active, your portrait, name, chat theme, and token image all swap to that persona's settings system-wide.

If you have no personas, you are always your true identity. Most characters never need more than one.

Add or edit personas from the picker pill in your dossier's cameo.

novel.disabledHint: The Novel Reader is disabled in your settings.

cover.titlePlaceholder: Pride and Prejudice. Emma. Persuasion. What is yours?
cover.emptyHint: The first letter, monologue, or session log will start the novel.

compose.intro: Open the composer in its own window to write a letter.

epistolary.sealedRow: A sealed letter — click to break the seal.
epistolary.markDone: Mark this character done with Epistolary

reader.breakSeal: Break the wax seal and open the letter

seal.yellowHint: Plain golden wax — for cheerful everyday correspondence.
seal.greenHint: Plain green wax — for unfussy notes.
seal.redGoldHint: Formal red wax pressed in gold. Logs an "invited" entry in the session log.
seal.copperHint: Burnished copper — fitting for matters of trade or finance.
seal.purpleHint: Deep purple wax — for formal but personal mail.
seal.blackHint: Black wax for matters meant to vanish. The letter destroys itself 30 seconds after the recipient opens it.
seal.goldKeyHint: An ornate gold-key impression — for secrets, promises, or covert correspondence.
seal.snakeHint: A coiled serpent — for matters of suspicion, intrigue, or counsel.
seal.owlHint: An owl in flight — for letters of wisdom, advice, or scholarship.
seal.sunHint: A bright sun — for joyful or congratulatory letters.
seal.mushroomHint: A toadstool — for letters of mischief or the curious naturalist.
seal.acornHint: An acorn — for letters of growth, beginnings, or pastoral matters.
seal.houseCloudcandleHint: The official seal of the House of Cloudcandle. Used for formal family correspondence.
seal.houseWillowoodHint: The official seal of the House of Willowood. Used for formal family correspondence.

phaseSplash.noActiveScene: No active scene — the splash is already showing.

step1.title: Upkeep
step1.prompt: Upkeep happens between cycles. Walk through these steps to wrap up this character.

step2.title: Tokens
step2.prompt: Refresh resolve tokens for this character.
step2.monologueNotTakenText: {name} has not yet taken their monologue this cycle. The token expires on advance.
step2.skipConfirm: Skip this step? Resolve will not be refreshed.

step3.title: Notes & Objectives
step3.prompt: What did this character think about during this cycle? What do they want to do next?
step3.placeholder: Write notes and plans for the next cycle...

step4.title: Desire
step4.prompt: Has this character's desire shifted, or does it still drive them?
step4.desireArchiveCard: {name} has set aside their desire "{oldDesire}" and now seeks "{newDesire}".
step4.desireEmptyWarning: Write a new desire before saving.
step4.desireSaveFailed: Could not save the new desire: {error}

step5.title: Reputation since the last Upkeep
step5.prompt: Review the reputation changes from this cycle. Acknowledge each one.
step5.noChanges: No reputation changes this cycle. Nothing to review.

step6.title: Upkeep complete.
step6.prompt: This character is ready for the next cycle.
step6.conflictActive: Inner conflict standing: {filled} of {total} boxes filled

upkeepWizard.disabled: The Upkeep Wizard is disabled in your settings.
upkeepWizard.noOwnedMajors: You don't own any Major characters to walk through Upkeep.

conditionPicker.prompt: This character has reached three tags of one polarity. Choose a Reputation Condition to mark it, or dismiss to handle it later.
conditionPicker.noConditions: No conditions available. Check that a conditions compendium is loaded.
conditionPicker.createError: Failed to create condition. See the console for details.

upkeepRoster.subtitle: Track everyone's progress before advancing.
upkeepRoster.advanceConfirm: {pending} player(s) haven't completed Upkeep. Advance anyway?
upkeepRoster.openWizardConfirm: Open Upkeep wizard for {name}? Changes will be attributed to you.

reputationPhaseWizard.playerToast: The Reputation phase has started. Your facilitator will lead the table.

rail.noneQualify: No characters qualify for this step.

step1.title: Reputation Phase · Roster
step1.prompt: Select which major characters are active in this cycle.
step1.noMajors: No major characters have been created yet.
step1.overview1: Create new reputation tags based on what each character did this chapter.
step1.overview2: Manage reputation conditions: add or clear them for each character.
step1.overview3: Post a summary and close the wizard.

step2.prompt: Review this character's criteria. What did they do this chapter?

criterion.desireVsDuty: Chose desire over duty or morality

step2.selectCharacter: Select a character from the list to begin.

step3.title: Manage Conditions
step3.prompt: Add or clear reputation conditions for {name}.
step3.selectCharacter: Select a character from the list.

step4.title: Phase Complete
step4.prompt: The Reputation Phase is complete. A summary will be posted to the chat log.
step4.advanceNote: Advance the cycle phase manually via the Cycle HUD when ready.

summary.heading: Reputation Phase complete. Where everyone stands:
summary.totals: {tagsCreated} new tag(s) this phase · {conditionsCleared} condition(s) cleared.

playerReputationPhaseWizard.noOwnedMajors: You have no major characters to review.

step1.title: Reputation Phase
step1.prompt: This wizard guides you through the Reputation phase for {name}. Review the steps before you begin.
step1.overview1: Review the reputation criteria and how {name} acted this chapter.
step1.overview2: Add or remove reputation tags based on what happened.
step1.overview3: Trigger any new conditions if you have 3 or more tags of the same type.
step1.overview4: Clear conditions that no longer apply.

step2.title: Criteria
step2.prompt: Did {name} meet any of these criteria this chapter? Reflect before adding tags in the next step.

criterion.desireVsDuty: Chose desire over duty or morality

step2.noFamily: No family linked. No unique criteria.

step3.title: Add Tags
step3.prompt: Add or remove reputation tags for {name} based on what happened this chapter.
step3.noTags: No tags yet. Add one below.

step4.title: Conditions
step4.triggerPrompt: {name} has {polarity} tags at or above the threshold.
step4.conditionClearedCard: {name}'s reputation condition "{condition}" has cleared. Their tag count dropped below 3.
step4.nothingToDo: No conditions to trigger or clear. Move on.

step5.title: Done
step5.prompt: You have completed the Reputation phase for {name}.
step5.completionCard: {name} completed the Reputation phase. Tags added: {tagsAdded}. Conditions triggered: {conditionsTriggered}. Conditions cleared: {conditionsCleared}.

eventTimeline.heading: Event Timeline
eventTimeline.deleteConfirm: Delete this event? This cannot be undone.
eventTimeline.emptyGM: No events yet. Add one below to begin building the timeline.
eventTimeline.emptyPlayer: No events have been shared yet.
eventTimeline.errorNoTitle: Please give the event a title.
eventTimeline.subtitle: Move events from Coming Soon to Today, link them to a scene, then conclude when the scene ends.
eventTimeline.dateLabelPlaceholder: next month, after the harvest, etc.
eventTimeline.revertHint: Move this event back to Coming Soon.
eventTimeline.revertConfirm: Move this event back to Coming Soon? Its scene link will be cleared.
eventTimeline.autoRevealedNotice: This event was hidden; players can now see it.

castSkill.revealBody: Casting {skill} will play a visible animation on your token. All players will see it. Proceed?

letterComposer.subject: Subject
letterComposer.previewLabel: PREVIEW · how the letter will appear in chat
letterComposer.noOwnedActors: No owned actors — ask your GM to assign ownership.
letterComposer.noRecipientActors: No recipients visible — ask your GM.
letterComposer.discardBody: You have unsaved changes. Discard the current letter?

rumourWizard.heading: Rumour & Scandal
rumourWizard.subtitleSetup: Pick the turn order, then begin.
rumourWizard.round1: Round 1: first pass around the circle
rumourWizard.setupHelp: Each included player will take two turns. The order reverses for round two. Use the arrows to reorder; uncheck a name to exclude.
rumourWizard.waitingForGMSetup: Waiting on the Facilitator to set up the turn order…
rumourWizard.yourTurn: ⚜  Your turn. Create a rumour, or spread one.
rumourWizard.actionSpreadHelp: Spreading costs your Major Character 1 resolve and attaches that token to the rumour. Anyone can later spend it during a novel chapter or epistolary phase.
rumourWizard.mustCreateNote: No spreadable rumours. Your only option is to create a new one.
rumourWizard.forceCompleteHint: GM only. Fast-forward to fade-out and close the wizard if the phase is stuck (e.g. a player disconnected mid-turn).
rumourWizard.forceCompleteConfirm: End the Rumour & Scandal phase now? Any unspread rumours will fade. This cannot be undone.

summary.heading: Rumour & Scandal phase complete. What circulates now:
summary.spreadHeading: Spread (still in play, each with a resolve token attached):
summary.fadingHeading: Created this phase but not yet spread (will fade if not spread next time):
summary.fadedHeading: Faded this phase (cycled out, no longer circulating):

rumourWizard.noRumoursYet: No rumours yet. The first turn always creates one.
rumourWizard.fadeoutHelp: Round 2 complete. Click below to fade unspread rumours and finish the phase.
rumourWizard.waitingForGMFadeout: Waiting on the Facilitator to finish the phase…
rumourWizard.completionCard: Rumour & Scandal phase complete. {n} rumours circulating.
rumourWizard.spreadCostBlocked: {name} has no resolve to spend. You must create a rumour instead.
rumourWizard.errorNoMajor: You don't own a Major Character to spend resolve from.

rumourBoard.heading: Rumours circulating in society
rumourBoard.subtitle: Spread rumours carry a resolve token until used. Click ● to spend it.
rumourBoard.useTokenHint: Spend the resolve token to twist the story.
rumourBoard.useConfirm: Spend this rumour’s resolve token? It will be marked used and crossed off.

eventPopover.strategiesHint: Write three approaches you might take to address this. Roll 1d20 for each — your average across the three rolls determines the outcome.
eventPopover.noTagOptions: No tag options were authored for this outcome. Submit to close — no tag will be created.
eventPopover.rollAllFirst: Roll all three strategies before resolving.
eventPopover.pickTagFirst: Pick a reputation tag before resolving.

randomEvent.noTag: No reputation change — this event carried no tag options.

eventCommandCenter.heading: Random events
eventCommandCenter.hint: Pick a Major character, then launch an archetype-pooled event. Players write three strategies, roll 1d20 each — average ≥ 11 succeeds.
eventCommandCenter.noMajors: No Major characters in this world yet.
eventCommandCenter.pickMajor: Select a Major character from the roster to view their archetype pool.
eventCommandCenter.noArchetypeWarn: This character has no archetype assigned. Open the dossier and pick one before launching an event.
eventCommandCenter.assignArchetypeFirst: This character has no archetype assigned. Open the dossier and pick one before launching an event.
eventCommandCenter.archetypeMismatch: This event's archetype does not match the selected character. Pick a matching event.
eventCommandCenter.ownerOffline: {name}'s player is offline — cannot deliver the event right now.
eventCommandCenter.offlineHint: No player owner is currently online.
eventCommandCenter.emptyPool: No events authored for this archetype yet. Create one to fill the pool.
eventCommandCenter.launchMissing: Could not launch — event or target not found.
eventCommandCenter.gmOnly: Only the GM can open the Event Command Center.
